definitions.text: To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.

examples: Tell me your likes and dislikes.

definitions.text: An individual vote showing support for, approval of, or enjoyment of, something posted on the Internet.

examples: My partner and I have like minds.
The two cats were as like as though they had come from the same litter.

definitions.text: In a like or similar manner.
(sometimes as the likes of) Someone similar to a given person, or something similar to a given object; a comparative; a type; a sort.
examples: There were bowls full of sweets, chocolates and the like.
This is something the likes of which I've never seen before, and we should compare like with like instead.

definitions.text: The stroke that equalizes the number of strokes played by the opposing player or side.
As if; as though; such as.
examples: It looks like you've finished the project.
It seemed like you didn't care.
Folk like that I just can’t brook.
It would be just like Achilles to be sulking in his tent.
Popcorn costs something like 10 dollars at the movies.
There were, like, twenty of them.

definitions.text: Used to precede an approximate quotation or paraphrase or an expression of something that happened.
examples: I was like, “Why did you do that?” and he's like, “I don't know.”
A customer walked in like, "I demand to see the manager!"
